videos.title: The Top 10 Carolina Hurricanes Moments in the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs
videos.summary_source_text:
The Top 10 Carolina Hurricanes Moments in the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs In honor of celebrating 10 Years of Locked On, we will be doing the Top 10 moments of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs for the Carolina Hurricanes. Between heated moments, comebacks, overtime goals, and lifting of the cup, which 10 moments stood out above the rest? On the Tuesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on selections 10 through 7 of the Top 10 Moments of the Hurricanes' 2026 playoff run. What moments stood out that just made the list, but fall just short of the top half? Secondly, Zach shares his selections 6 through 4 as we get closer to the Top 3. While they weren't the elite moments in the playoffs, which ones stood out to be closer to the Top 3 than falling in the back half of the Top 10? Finally, Zach shares his Top 3 moments of the Stanley Cup Playoffs for the Hurricanes. While it might be obvious what the top choice is, what moments came in the second and third spots? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

Kimi K 2.7 Code High-Speed Update: Quality vs Fast vs No-Think (Real Speed Tests)

Kimi K 2.7 Code has a new high-speed rollout with three “gears” for the same coding model: Quality (thinking on), Fast (thinking on but served faster), and No Think (thinking steps off for maximum speed but sometimes lower quality). The video compares side-by-side outputs and response times on three prompts—building a 3D solar system, generating a playable game, and creating a spiral galaxy—showing mixed results: some tasks are faster with No Think (e.g., 98s vs 53s vs 39s), while others can be slower, depending on what gets built. The script explains how thinking-on uses a hidden scratchpad while thinking-off writes immediately, and notes Moonshot’s quoted ~180–260 tokens/sec serving. It also demonstrates toggling modes in KimiCode within an Agent OS setup and promotes the AI Profit Boardroom for tutorials, tools, and coaching.

videos.title: NEW AMG GLE 63 REVEALED and it’s WEIRD…
videos.summary_source_text:
NEW AMG GLE 63 REVEALED and it’s WEIRD… Mercedes has unveiled the updated Mercedes-AMG GLE 63 & GLS 63, and we’re about to see what’s changed in terms of design, interior and performance. We’ll also discuss how much we think it will cost. 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-16-06

The AMG GLE 63 & GLS 63 have had mid-life facelifts! 

It's been three years since Mercedes last updated the cars that were originally released in 2018 & 2019 respectively, so let's see what's changed...

In terms of the exterior design, the cars still look relatively similar to the previous editions, with a few tweaks here and there. Up front there’s a bigger & bolder grille (which lights up at night), flanked on either side by headlights featuring massive 3-pointed stars, just like the AMG GT 4-door. The side profile on both cars is almost identical to the previous car, while the taillights feature even more 3-pointed star graphics, and as before, the four massive exhaust pipes have been retained for each car. 

On the inside, there’s one key difference - the huge wraparound screen that stretches across the dash, featuring the digital dials, infotainment screen, and passenger screen! It’s the same as found in the non-AMG editions of these cars, although here there are some different graphics. The seats come with additional supports, exclusive leather, and there’s a very similar steering wheel to that found in the AMG GT. 

When you peek under the bonnet, you’ll find a 4-litre twin-turbo V8, just as you did in the previous editions! However, in this instance, the engine has been given a new flat-plane crank! It’s the sort of thing you’d find in high-revving, naturally-aspirated V8s, however, don’t expect any additional power - it still produces 612hp & 850Nm, just as before. 

We don’t yet know the price, but we can guarantee one thing - neither of these cars will come cheap! The cheaper option will be the GLE 63, but don’t expect the new one to cost any less than an eye-watering £140,000!! 

So are you impressed with the upgrades to these top-of-the-range SUVs? Let us know in the comments! How to Get Fable 5-Level Intelligence Without Fable 5: Plan With a Frontier Model, Build Cheap

The episode explains how to approximate “Fable 5-level” results despite Fable 5 being taken down by using a two-phase workflow: have a frontier model create a highly pinned, decision-complete plan, then switch to a cheaper (or even free) model to build from that plan, with optional review agents for security and logic. Citing a Kilo case study, it claims identical services can be produced when different models implement the same pinned plan, with major token and cost reductions—planning with Claude Fable 5 and implementing with GPT 5.5 reportedly cut costs by 59% while still passing 15 acceptance checks. The script also notes routing plans across models if one is removed, reusing plans as assets, and mentions Fusion as another multi-model approach, plus resources available inside the AI Profit Boardroom.

videos.title: 15.06.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
15.06.26 - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

But over in Chicago, a team who lost a record 121 games in 2024, are leading their division just two years later. Can the Rockies rebuild resemble the White Sox resurgence? videos.title: Off-Season Under Way | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Off-Season Under Way | Morning Cuppa Hockey Morning Cuppa Hockey returns with Carolina now Stanley Cup champions, and our two guests are bringing very different lenses to the offseason chaos.

Drew Shore joins the show as a retired NHL forward turned player agent and host of the Betting The Limit podcast, bringing his perspective on what a thin July 1st free agent class means for the players actually navigating it, while Dan Bylsma comes in with the rare vantage point of having coached Jordan Staal as a young player on that 2009 Penguins Cup team and having worked directly with both Dylan Larkin and Anthony Mantha during his time in Detroit.

Jonny Lazarus and Colby Cohen dig into Jordan Staal's historic Conn Smythe run at 37 years old, what Larkin's trade request really says about where things stand in Detroit, whether Mantha's monster platform year actually translates into the contract he wants, and where Edmonton's coaching search and the Babcock rumors fit into the bigger picture of an NHL offseason that's already off to a wild start.

Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

videos.summary_source_text:

Hermes Just Added Async Sub-Agents: Delegate Tasks in the Background (No More Frozen Chat)

Hermes now supports asynchronous sub-agents via a new Delegate tool that can spawn multiple workers at once, running jobs in the background so chat no longer freezes. After updating Hermes (via terminal command or dashboard), you can give a lead Hermes agent a single goal and have it fan out tasks to temporary sub-agents, each with its own thread, goal, and context, dispatched in about two milliseconds using delegate task with background=true. Results return to the chat as each worker finishes, enabling parallel workflows like generating and deploying multiple SEO articles from one keyword. The script compares the old blocking, sequential approach to the new parallel “hive” model, notes you can use plain English, and mentions using different APIs per worker. It also promotes the AI Profit Boardroom’s agent operating system, training, roadmap, community, and coaching calls.

videos.title: Hindi - 15.06.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi - 15.06.26 - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: Looking Back at 10 Key Avs Moments to Celebrate the Locked on Podcast Network's 10th Anniversary!
videos.summary_source_text:
Looking Back at 10 Key Avs Moments to Celebrate the Locked on Podcast Network's 10th Anniversary! Colorado Avalanche fans relive a decade of unforgettable moments as the Locked On Podcast Network celebrates its 10th anniversary. Which Avalanche memory stands above all—Gabriel Landeskog’s emotional return from a groundbreaking injury, or the 2022 Stanley Cup triumph? Shocking trades, like the departure of Mikko Rantanen, and powerhouse playoff goals from Nazem Kadri and Darren Helm keep the debate fierce.

Hosts Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson highlight the impact of outdoor NHL games, key trade deadline returns, Peter McNab’s legacy behind the mic, and the franchise-altering drafting of Cale Makar. From coaching shakeups to the electric Game 82 win over the St. Louis Blues, every pivotal Avalanche milestone is on the table. Which iconic play or player should top the list, and what memories still ignite Mile High passion? Dive into the ultimate Colorado Avalanche countdown.

videos.title: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking
videos.summary_source_text:
Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking Google DeepMind just dropped a massive paper called From AGI to ASI, and the message is bigger than another AI release. The paper argues that AGI may not be the finish line everyone is waiting for. It may be the moment the real race begins. Once human-level AI can be copied, sped up, connected into agent teams, and used to build better AI, the jump after AGI could matter even more than AGI itself.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

Google DeepMind’s new From AGI to ASI paper

The full technical paper on arXiv
SOURCE: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12683
DeepMind’s earlier framework for measuring AGI progress
SOURCE: https://deepmind.google/research/publications/66938/
Google’s 2026 framework for tracking progress toward AGI
SOURCE: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-deepmind/measuring-agi-cognitive-framework/
DeepMind’s approach to AGI safety and security
SOURCE: https://deepmind.google/blog/taking-a-responsible-path-to-agi/
Demis Hassabis on AI agents and the road to AGI
SOURCE: https://www.axios.com/2026/05/26/deepmind-ceo-demis-hassabis
The Legg and Hutter paper behind formal machine intelligence
SOURCE: https://arxiv.org/abs/cs/0605024

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than another AI paper. Google DeepMind is already talking about what happens after AGI. If human-level AI can be copied, sped up, connected, and used to build better AI, then intelligence itself could become an industrial process.

SOURCE: https://deepmind.google/research/publications/239142/
The full technical paper on arXiv
SOURCE: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12683
DeepMind’s earlier framework for measuring AGI progress
SOURCE: https://deepmind.google/research/publications/66938/
Google’s 2026 framework for tracking progress toward AGI
SOURCE: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-deepmind/measuring-agi-cognitive-framework/
DeepMind’s approach to AGI safety and security
SOURCE: https://deepmind.google/blog/taking-a-responsible-path-to-agi/
Demis Hassabis on AI agents and the road to AGI
SOURCE: https://www.axios.com/2026/05/26/deepmind-ceo-demis-hassabis
The Legg and Hutter paper behind formal machine intelligence
SOURCE: https://arxiv.org/abs/cs/0605024

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than another AI paper. Google DeepMind is already talking about what happens after AGI. If human-level AI can be copied, sped up, connected, and used to build better AI, then intelligence itself could become an industrial process.

#ai #agi #deepmind
videos.summary: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking Google DeepMind just dropped a massive paper called From AGI to ASI, and the message is bigger than another AI release. The paper argues that AGI may not be the finish line everyone is waiting for.

videos.title: How Coach Prime and Colorado FLIPPED an SEC commit during another big recruiting weekend
videos.summary_source_text:
How Coach Prime and Colorado FLIPPED an SEC commit during another big recruiting weekend On today’s DNVR Buffs Podcast, the squad discusses the three recruits who committed to Coach Prime and Colorado after another successful weekend of official visits. Three-star EDGE Drew Sapp finally committed to Colorado and three-star offensive tackle Coderro McDaniel flipped his commitment from Ole Miss to Colorado. The Buffs also secured another Talib, interior offensive lineman Jayin Talib also committed to Colorado over the weekend. Ryan Koenigsberg, Jake Schwanitz and Scott Procter discuss another successful recruiting weekend, plus the latest on Brendan Sorsby, including Coach Prime and the Big 12’s response to the controversy. Why some schools have a “no visit” policy, drafting CFP Dark Horse candidates and much more.

videos.title: Why Modern Life Keeps You Stressed
videos.summary_source_text: Why Modern Life Keeps You Stressed Modern life keeps many people stuck in a constant state of stress. The nervous system was designed to handle short bursts of danger, not endless emails, deadlines, notifications, and worries. Over time, that constant activation can become the default setting. Learning how to stimulate the vagus nerve through slow, intentional breathing may be one of the simplest ways to restore balance and quiet the stress response. Modern life keeps many people stuck in a constant state of stress. The nervous system was designed to handle short bursts of danger, not endless emails, deadlines, notifications, and worries. Over time, that constant activation can become the default setting. Learning how to stimulate the vagus nerve through slow, intentional breathing may be one of the simplest ways to restore balance and quiet the stress response.

videos.title: GPT-5.5 vs Claude Fable 5: Which AI Model Is Worth the Price?
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT-5.5 vs Claude Fable 5: Which AI Model Is Worth the Price? Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/

Is Claude Fable 5 or GPT-5.5 actually better — or is the whole debate just marketing? I ran both models against the same tasks, paid for both out of pocket, and broke down the numbers the vendors don't highlight.

In this video: architecture differences, real benchmark results (SWE-Bench, reasoning evals, hallucination rates), actual API pricing, and a clear use-case framework so you know which model to reach for — and when.

⏱ CHAPTERS
00:00:58 Architecture & Features
00:02:05 Coding
00:03:07 Reasoning & General QA
00:03:48 Vision & Multimodal
00:04:20 Hallucinations & Safety
00:05:10 Pricing & Throughput
00:05:58 Fine-Tuning & Customization
00:06:38 Ecosystem & Integrations
00:07:22 Use-Case Recommendations
00:08:21 Strengths & Weaknesses Recap


🔑 KEY TAKEAWAYS
- Claude Fable 5 leads on coding (SWE-Bench ~80% vs ~59%), long-context tasks, and hallucination rate
- GPT-5.5 leads on ecosystem integrations, speed, and cost (~half the price per token)
- Neither wins everything — the right answer depends on your context window needs, budget, and how costly a wrong answer is
- The smartest move: route by task, not by loyalty

📌 SOURCES & MODEL CARDS
[Link to sources]

🔔 Subscribe for AI comparisons without the launch-week hype — BitBiased.

It showcases multiple outputs (a dark first-person game, clean websites, 3D/MMO/RPG-style demos, animations like glowing fish trails and lava-lamp liquid that follows the mouse) while noting quality is inconsistent and sometimes disconnected from prompts. The creator compares Grok Build against Kimi Code, GLM 5.2, and Claude Opus, claiming Grok Build’s game beat Claude’s for the same prompt and outperformed Kimi and GLM on some tests, though GLM looked better for certain animations. They highlight OAuth login and that X Premium Plus/Super Grok subscribers get access, plus parallel sub-agents, self-check loops, and integration into an agent operating system alongside other models, then promote an Agent OS package via AI Profit Bot.

videos.title: Varför bankkontot är riskfyllt på lång sikt
videos.summary_source_text:
Varför bankkontot är riskfyllt på lång sikt Bankkontot känns tryggt — men det är en garanterad förlust om du låter pengarna stanna där för länge.

Jag går igenom hur inflation urholkar köpkraften (Riksbankens mål: 2% per år), varför priserna ökade med nästan 23 procent mellan 2021 och 2025, och hur du bör tänka kring kortsiktiga kontra långsiktiga pengar.

Tumregel: 0–3 år på konto, längre än så — investera.

Mer på rikatillsammans.se — länk i pinnad kommentar.

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4nVGCE7

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

In this video, you’ll learn a simple at-home hydration test that can help you quickly determine if your body is low on fluids. All it takes is a quick skin pinch test to check your skin turgor, a simple indicator of hydration levels and fluid balance underneath the skin.

Watch until the end to learn what your skin may be telling you about your hydration status and why proper hydration is about more than just drinking water.

videos.title: The #1 Warning Sign Your Body Is Begging for Magnesium | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
The #1 Warning Sign Your Body Is Begging for Magnesium | Dr. Mandell Could your body be trying to tell you that it’s low in magnesium? In this video, Dr. Mandell explains one of the most overlooked mineral deficiencies and how it may quietly affect your sleep, energy, muscles, brain, heart, digestion, and overall health. Discover why magnesium is essential for over 300 biochemical reactions, how it supports ATP production, nerve function, muscle relaxation, healthy blood pressure, blood sugar balance, and restorative sleep, and why the earliest warning signs are often ignored. Learn the common symptoms of low magnesium, the best food sources to naturally increase your intake, the differences between popular forms of magnesium supplements, and why a normal blood test doesn’t always tell the whole story. Your body often whispers before it screams, and recognizing these subtle clues could make a tremendous difference in your health and quality of life. Watch until the end to understand how one simple mineral may impact virtually every cell in your body and what you can do today to support optimal wellness naturally.

videos.title: Carolina Hurricanes are 2026 Stanley Cup Champions ft. Aaron Ward, Mike Maniscalco, & Greg Wyshynski
videos.summary_source_text:
Carolina Hurricanes are 2026 Stanley Cup Champions ft. Aaron Ward, Mike Maniscalco, & Greg Wyshynski The Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup Champions! 🏆

On a special championship edition of MVSW, Jeff Marek and Greg Wyshynski react to the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 6 to capture the 2026 Stanley Cup, their first championship since 2006. The guys break down the defining moments of Carolina's Cup run, Jordan Staal's Conn Smythe-winning performance, Rod Brind'Amour's incredible journey from Cup-winning captain to Stanley Cup-winning head coach, and what this championship means for the Hurricanes franchise moving forward. Carolina secured the Cup with a 3-0 victory over Vegas, capping off a dominant playoff run and earning the organization's second Stanley Cup in franchise history.

Joining the show is former Hurricanes Stanley Cup champion Aaron Ward, who reflects on the similarities and differences between the 2006 and 2026 championship teams, the culture that helped build a winner in Carolina, and the significance of bringing another Stanley Cup to Raleigh. Then, Hurricanes play-by-play voice Mike Maniscalco joins live from the celebration to share his perspective from inside the organization, discuss the atmosphere surrounding the championship victory, and look back at the key moments that defined Carolina's unforgettable postseason run.

All that and more on a special Stanley Cup-winning edition of The Sheet with Jeff Marek and Greg Wyshynski.

videos.summary_source_text:
The Backswing Mistake That Keeps Ruining Your Irons ⛳️ Take Your FREE Game Assessment → https://meandmygolf.com/game-plan/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=15.06.26

📱 Download the Swing Coach App (2-Week Free Trial) → https://go.meandmygolf.com/swingcoach/yt/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=15.06.26

If your irons feel inconsistent, the strike isn’t clean, and accuracy comes and goes, the problem may have started much earlier in the swing than you think.

In this lesson, we worked with Matt to uncover the backswing mistake that was leading to poor iron shots, steep delivery, and unreliable contact. Once we changed that pattern, everything started to improve: better strikes, better height, and much straighter shots.

This is a great lesson for any golfer who feels like their irons can look good one day and disappear the next. If that sounds familiar, this will help you understand what may really be going on.

videos.title: 25 Years of Fitness Advice in 60 Seconds
videos.summary_source_text:
25 Years of Fitness Advice in 60 Seconds What would 25 years of fitness advice sound like if you had to fit it into just 60 seconds?

I'm going to break down some of the biggest lessons learned from decades of training, coaching, physical therapy, injury prevention, muscle building, fat loss, and helping people train smarter for life.

The truth is, most people overcomplicate fitness. They chase the perfect workout split, the perfect diet, the perfect supplement stack, or the perfect exercise, when the real answers are usually much simpler. That does not mean they are easy. Nutrition is simple, but staying consistent with it is not. The workout may only take one hour, but the other 23 hours of your day determine whether your results actually show up.

You will learn why injuries should not be treated like a total shutdown, why changing your workout split can restart progress, why training to failure can be one of the most objective ways to stimulate muscle growth, and why recovery is the thing that determines whether failure actually works.

I also cover the difference between muscle soreness and joint soreness, why tight muscles often need better full range of motion instead of endless stretching, and why lifting a weight you cannot control is usually a sign that it is too heavy. Training hard matters, but training smart matters even more, especially as you get older.

This short also touches on some of the most misunderstood nutrition and supplement topics, including carbs, calories, protein, creatine, omega-3s, and why most supplements are optional while a few can make a meaningful difference when your training and diet are already in place.

If your goal is to build muscle, burn fat, get stronger, avoid injury, and stay consistent for the long run, these are the fitness rules that matter most.

For more fitness tips and education, be sure to stay tuned to this channel and remember to subscribe so that you never miss another video from a physical therapist with a pro sports background as a PT and strength coach.

You can subscribe to this channel here - http://bit.ly/2b0coMW

Get Your Workouts Here:
📝 https://www.athleanx.com

Connect With Me:
👉 https://www.instagram.com/athleanx 

For complete step-by-step workout programs, head on over to athleanx.com and make sure to use the program selector to find the training plan that is best suited to your personal goals.

Jeff Cavaliere MSPT, CSCS served as both the head physical therapist and assistant strength coach for the New York Mets. Jeff earned his Masters of Physical Therapy and Bachelor’s of Physioneurobiology from the College of Health Sciences University of Connecticut Storrs. He is a certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist by the National Strength and Conditioning Association (NSCA). Jeff Cavaliere MSPT, CSCS served as both the head physical therapist and assistant strength coach for the New York Mets. Jeff earned his Masters of Physical Therapy and Bachelor’s of Physioneurobiology from the College of Health Sciences University of Connecticut Storrs. He is a certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist by the National Strength and Conditioning Association (NSCA).

Why Denver Broncos OC Davis Webb sees Josh Allen & Stefon Diggs in Bo Nix & Jaylen Waddle Denver Broncos offensive coordinator Davis Webb sees shades of Josh Allen and Stefon Diggs in the potential connection between Bo Nix and Jaylen Waddle. Could this be the next elite quarterback-wide receiver duo in the NFL?

The DNVR Broncos Crew breaks down Webb’s comparison, what it says about Bo Nix’s development, how Jaylen Waddle could fit in Sean Payton’s offense, and whether the Denver Broncos are building a passing attack capable of competing with the AFC’s best. Plus, what else did Davis Webb have to say in his first media appearance since Sean Payton made him the Denver Broncos’ offensive coordinator?

The guys discuss Bo Nix’s growth entering a pivotal season, Davis Webb’s influence on the Broncos’ offense, Sean Payton’s vision for Denver, and what a true No. 1 receiver could mean for the future of the franchise.

videos.title: Claude 6: What's Actually True, What's Hype, and Why the Government Shut It Down
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude 6: What's Actually True, What's Hype, and Why the Government Shut It Down Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/

Every few weeks, the AI industry drops another “this changes everything” model — and the latest wave around Anthropic’s next-generation Claude system is no different. But behind the hype, benchmarks, and leaked-sounding claims, there’s a much messier story: unclear model naming, questionable performance narratives, safety systems that quietly reroute your prompts, and a government intervention that no one fully agrees on yet.

In this breakdown, I cut through the noise and walk you through what’s actually real, what’s marketing spin, and what’s straight-up speculation in the so-called “Claude 6” conversation. We look at the real Claude lineup, what benchmarks actually tell us (and what they don’t), how safety routing systems may change your experience without you noticing, and whether any of this is truly approaching AGI — or just faster pattern recognition with better branding.

🔥 TIMESTAMPS
00:01:36 What Claude 6 Is Actually Built On
00:03:12 The Numbers: Verify vs Vendor
00:05:58 The Safety System Nobody Voted For
00:07:11 The Government Recall
00:09:13 The AGI Question
00:10:44 What Claude 6 Could Realistically Be

📌 KEY TAKEAWAYS

“Claude 6” is not an official Anthropic release — it’s speculation, not a product
Many viral benchmark claims are either self-reported or non-verifiable
Real Claude models rely on Constitutional AI + layered safety systems
Some safety systems may route or filter queries, but not in the dramatic “secret model swap” way often described
Government AI regulation exists, but the “full model recall” narrative is highly questionable
The real story isn’t AGI — it’s incremental capability gains wrapped in aggressive marketing cycles

💬 I want to hear from you:
→ Do you think AI companies are getting more transparent or less?
→ Where do you personally draw the line between hype and reality?
→ Are we improving intelligence — or just scaling better autocomplete?

Drop your take in the comments — I read them.

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for deep, no-hype breakdowns of every major AI release — separating real progress from marketing noise.

videos.title: Can I Break the Record at a Public Golf Course? (One Chance)
videos.summary_source_text:
Can I Break the Record at a Public Golf Course? (One Chance) Sometimes with public courses, you never know exactly what you’re going to get. A lot of people give them a bad rap, but today, we’re playing one of the special ones. I’m at the historic Tenison Park Golf Course in Dallas, Texas, where golf legend Lee Trevino spent years hustling and perfecting his game. The course is in great condition and today I’m attempting to break the course record in only one try. This is going to be a solid test of golf, and hopefully it gets me one step closer to my goal of breaking three course records by the end of the year...

Shoutout to Tenison Park Golf Course for hosting me!

videos.title: Storm Surgin’ With Stanley: Canes are Champions | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Storm Surgin’ With Stanley: Canes are Champions | Daily Faceoff Live The Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions for the second time in franchise history, capping off a dominant postseason run with a 3-0 victory over the Vegas Golden Knights. After years of knocking on the door, Rod Brind’Amour’s group finally broke through and cemented themselves as the NHL’s gold standard.

Tyler Yaremchuk and Colby Cohen break down Carolina’s championship-clinching performance, the incredible story of Brandon Bussi stepping in and delivering when the Hurricanes needed him most, and why no team has been more consistently successful over the last eight seasons. The guys also discuss Jordan Staal’s leadership, loyalty to the organization, and historic Conn Smythe Trophy victory as the oldest player in NHL history to win playoff MVP.

Was this the crowning achievement that solidifies Rod Brind’Amour as a Hall of Fame coach? What can the rest of the NHL learn from the Hurricanes' blueprint for success? And which member of the championship team has the best story?

Plus, what comes next for the Vegas Golden Knights after another deep playoff run? We examine their offseason priorities, key contract decisions, and whether this roster has another Stanley Cup Final appearance in it.

David Pagnotta joins the show to share his thoughts on the Hurricanes' championship, the Conn Smythe debate, Vegas’ future, and the biggest storylines coming out of the Stanley Cup Final.

videos.summary_source_text:

Your trail arm plays a much bigger role in your golf swing than most golfers realise — and if it’s not working correctly, it can lead to weak shots, poor contact, and inconsistency.

In this video, I break down exactly how the trail arm should be moving in the golf swing and why so many golfers get it wrong without even knowing it. Once you understand this movement, it becomes much easier to create better structure, more control, and stronger ball striking.

This is one of the most important but misunderstood parts of the swing, and small changes here can have a huge impact on your overall performance.

If you’ve been struggling with inconsistent shots, this is a simple place to start.

This is one of the most important but misunderstood parts of the swing, and small changes here can have a huge impact on your overall performance.

If you’ve been struggling with inconsistent shots, this is a simple place to start.

videos.title: Z-Wave Explained: Is It Still Worth Using?
videos.summary_source_text:
Z-Wave Explained: Is It Still Worth Using? Homey supports Z-Wave. Here’s how it works, where it still makes sense, and what to watch for.

Z-Wave is a low-power smart home protocol that runs on sub-GHz frequencies, communicates locally, and is built around certified interoperability. That gives it real strengths throughout the home, especially for battery-powered sensors and locks that need to last for years.

It also has trade-offs: lower data speeds, regional frequency differences, higher device costs, and devices that can only belong to one controller at a time.

This video covers Z-Wave’s history, mesh networking, Z-Wave Long Range, S2 security, device types, direct associations, and how Z-Wave compares with protocols like Zigbee and Wi-Fi.

videos.title: Carolina Hurricanes: Stanley Cup Champions | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Carolina Hurricanes: Stanley Cup Champions | Morning Cuppa Hockey Season 3, Episode 148 of Morning Cuppa Hockey. The Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions. Jonny Lazarus and Colby Cohen tip their cups to the Canes in the first half of the show before Adam Gold — the voice of Hurricanes hockey on 99.9 The Fan in Raleigh and host of Canes Corner — joins to break it all down.

Carolina capped a 16-3 postseason with a 3-0 shutout of the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 6 of the Stanley Cup Final Sunday night, claiming Lord Stanley's chalice for the second time in franchise history. Brandon Bussi was immaculate between the pipes, stopping all 22 shots he faced, while Jackson Blake, Taylor Hall, and Nikolaj Ehlers all found the back of the net. 

Captain Jordan Staal took home the Conn Smythe Trophy after leading all skaters with six goals in the Final — scoring in each of the first five games — becoming the oldest playoff MVP in NHL history in the process. Rod Brind'Amour, who lifted the Cup as Carolina's captain back in 2006, became just the seventh person in NHL history to win a championship with the same franchise as both a player and a coach. 

Adam Gold has been embedded with this fanbase through every heartbreaking conference final exit, and today he joins Jonny and Colby to talk about what this moment means for Raleigh, the legacy of this team, and why a 37-year-old two-way centre just had the most clutch playoff run of the entire season.

Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

videos.title: Pádraig Harrington: How I Won 3 Majors in 13 Months
videos.summary_source_text:
Pádraig Harrington: How I Won 3 Majors in 13 Months Pádraig Harrington joins George Harper Jr. at The K Club in Ireland for this episode of the Life on Tour podcast, presented by Buffalo Trace, for an insightful and entertaining conversation with one of the most accomplished players in DP World Tour history. With 15 DP World Tour victories and more than 500 tournaments under his belt, Pádraig reflects on a career spent competing at the very highest level and takes listeners inside his remarkable run of three Major Championship victories in just 13 months. He reveals the preparation system, mindset and practice routine that helped him reach the pinnacle of the game.

Pádraig also shares how he celebrated his Open Championship victories, what it was really like going head-to-head with Tiger Woods during golf’s most dominant era, and the lessons he learned from performing under the greatest pressure.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

videos.title: The Path to Iron Compression: Simple Drills For Instant Results
videos.summary_source_text:
The Path to Iron Compression: Simple Drills For Instant Results Stop trying to scoop your golf ball up into the air! If you want elite-level low point control and that pure iron compression that better players talk about, you don't need a complicated golf swing. You need 5 minutes a day to fix your impact position.

In this video, we build a step-by-step roadmap to transform your iron play using simple golf drills you can do at home, in the garden, or on the driving range.

What you will learn in this golf lesson:

The Two-Tee Low Point Control Drill: How to stop hitting behind the ball and shift your low point forward for crisp, pure strikes.
The Closed Face Control Drill: A unique practical drill to fix your clubface orientation, improve your wrist angles, and create effortless shaft lean without overthinking your swing positions.
The "Turning the Corner" Secret: Why trying too hard to shift your hips forward actually causes a "flicky," scoopy impact—and the simple one-foot drill to fix it.

Grab an 8-iron and two golf tees, and let's fix your golf swing mechanics so you can start compressing your irons today!

FREE Golf Swing 101 Class here: https://www.markcrossfieldgolf.com/how-to-play-golf-101

videos.title: Carolina's Stanley Cup Triumph + Tortorella, Vegas & NHL Offseason Buzz | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
Carolina's Stanley Cup Triumph + Tortorella, Vegas & NHL Offseason Buzz | DFO Rundown The Carolina Hurricanes are Stanley Cup champions for the first time in 20 years, and Jason Gregor and Jeff Marek break down how one of the NHL's deepest and most complete teams finally got over the hump.

The guys discuss Jordan Staal's leadership, Taylor Hall's remarkable journey to a Stanley Cup, Rod Brind'Amour's impact behind the bench, and why Eric Tulsky deserves massive credit for building a championship roster. They also examine Carolina's offseason outlook, including the futures of Frederik Andersen, Nick Deslauriers, and several key contributors.

Then the focus shifts to the Vegas Golden Knights. After an impressive run to the Stanley Cup Final, what comes next? Could Ryan Craig replace John Tortorella behind the bench? What does Pavel Dorofeyev's next contract look like? Has Rasmus Andersson's playoff performance changed anything regarding a potential extension? And what should Vegas prioritize this summer?

We then talk John Tortorella's coaching future, Carolina's offseason plans, whether another NHL star could request a trade this summer, offer sheet candidates including Maverick Bourque and Pavel Dorofeyev, the Chicago Blackhawks' next step in their rebuild, and why Darren Raddysh could be one of the most intriguing free agents on the market.

videos.title: Melt Cellulite by Doing This 3X a Week (No Squats Needed)
videos.summary_source_text:
Melt Cellulite by Doing This 3X a Week (No Squats Needed) The best exercise for cellulite isn’t running, squats, or traditional cardio. Cellulite is often linked to connective tissue, inflammation, and high insulin, not just body fat.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4xswdnF

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

To target cellulite from both angles:
• High-intensity interval training (HIIT)
• Low-carb diet

Just 20 minutes of sprints or cycling, three times per week, combined with lowering insulin through diet, can be more effective than hours of cardio.

videos.title: 469. Pengar, ansvar och RikaTillsammans bakom kulisserna | Del 2 av 2
videos.summary_source_text:
469. Pengar, ansvar och RikaTillsammans bakom kulisserna | Del 2 av 2 Varför väljer jag index trots att jag kan aktier? Varför har vi aldrig velat förvalta andras pengar? Och vad händer när ett hobbyprojekt växer till något som vissa månader når sju procent av Sveriges befolkning?

I den här personliga uppföljningen till avsnittet om pengar, rädslor och bristtänk pratar jag, Caroline och vår kollega Oliver om livet bakom RikaTillsammans. Om identitet, ansvar, status, relationer och de lärdomar som följt med när ett sidoprojekt blivit en stor del av livet.

Vi pratar om varför jag en gång byggde min identitet kring att slå marknaden, varför jag i dag föredrar indexfonder, varför vi valt bort både rådgivning och kapitalförvaltning samt hur vi tänker kring pengar, konsumtion och ett rikt liv.

Det här är ett av våra mest personliga samtal hittills. Hoppas du gillar det.

30+ Yards More! The Slingshot Golf Swing ft JT Thomas (2026 Version) In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas show how to hit driver farther by generating speed from your arms and hands.

⏱️ Timestamps:

If you've been trying to gain driver distance by rotating harder and spinning your hips faster — this video will change how you think about the golf swing. Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas break down why arm and hand speed is the highest return on investment for golfers who want more distance with less effort. The whoosh drill and impact bag drill are practical golf drills any golfer can use to feel what real club head speed is supposed to feel like. If you leave this video without trying the whoosh drill, you're leaving distance on the table.

What You'll Learn:
• Why moving the body and club at the same rate destroys driver distance
• The "all gas no brakes" mistake most golfers make and how to fix it
• How to generate speed from your arms and hands for more effortless power
• The whoosh drill and how to use it to train real club head speed
• How the impact bag drill teaches you to swing your arms faster through the hitting zone
• Why slowing the lead side down actually makes the club go faster
• How wrist motion — down, forward, and away from the body — generates speed
• Why senior golfers get the biggest distance gains from arm and hand speed
• Golf tips any golfer can use to learn how to hit driver farther and play better golf

videos.title: Från pilot till affärsvärde – så lyckas ledande bolag med AI
videos.summary_source_text:
Från pilot till affärsvärde – så lyckas ledande bolag med AI AI används brett i dag, men bara 20 % av företagen skapar majoriteten av affärsvärdet. Varför fastnar så många i pilotstadiet – och vad krävs för att lyckas fullt ut?

Lyssna in till denna LinkedIn Live där PwC:s Måns Liljenlov och Andreas Håkansson, AI Lead, delar insikter från AI Performance Study och ger konkreta perspektiv på hur företag kan gå från initiativ till verkligt affärsvärde.

Ta del av undersökningen här: https://www.pwc.se/sv/ai/ai-performance-study-2026.pdf AI används brett i dag, men bara 20 % av företagen skapar majoriteten av affärsvärdet. Varför fastnar så många i pilotstadiet – och vad krävs för att lyckas fullt ut?

Lyssna in till denna LinkedIn Live där PwC:s Måns Liljenlov och Andreas Håkansson, AI Lead, delar insikter från AI Performance Study och ger konkreta perspektiv på hur företag kan gå från initiativ till verkligt affärsvärde.

videos.title: My golf club store room is pretty special 😍
videos.summary_source_text:
My golf club store room is pretty special 😍 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook

• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball

• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

videos.title: Has Hamilton Put Ferrari In The Fight? | Barcelona-Catalunya GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Has Hamilton Put Ferrari In The Fight? | Barcelona-Catalunya GP Review | F1 Nation Podcast Tom Clarkson is joined by ESPN’s Albert Fabrega and Brazilian journalist Julianne Cerasoli to reflect on Lewis Hamilton’s first Grand Prix victory for Ferrari in Barcelona.

What does this victory tell us about Lewis after such a challenging first year with Ferrari? Why did their three-stop strategy get the better of Mercedes? And are Ferrari now a big threat to them in the title race?

You’ll hear reaction from Hamilton and Ferrari Team Principal Fred Vasseur. 

George Russell came home in second place, closing the gap to teammate and championship leader Kimi Antonelli after the Italian suffered a late retirement. But after starting on pole and being the lead Mercedes driver for the majority of the race, George was overtaken by Kimi late on before his DNF. So while George did make some much-needed progress, will he leave Barcelona worried? And how costly could Mercedes’ continuing reliability issues be in their season?

00:00 - History for Hamilton
10:54 - Fred Vasseur on Lewis' 'milestone'
19:01 - Leclerc's 'important' change
23:07 - Will George be worried?
32:47 - McLaren's mixed performance
40:58 - Alpine + RB in the points
43:17 - Driver of the Day?

videos.title: Why is Europe SCARED of CHINESE cars! Chery Tiggo 4 Review
videos.summary_source_text:
Why is Europe SCARED of CHINESE cars! Chery Tiggo 4 Review This is our in-depth review of the all-new Chery TIGGO 4. We’re reviewing the interior design, space and practicality, finding out what it’s like to drive, and reviewing how it performs in a 0-60mph & brake test. 

Get 20% off Car Vertical using code Carwow: https://bit.ly/Carwow-Car-Vertical

This is the all-new Chery Tiggo 4! 

Mat’s got his hands on this affordable family car to see if this latest offering from China has enough to sway you away from more established competitors like the Renault Captur and Volkswagen T-Roc. 

So let’s get into it, starting with the design. 

On first glance, it’s easy to see that the Tiggo 4 designers have taken inspiration from several other cars. The front end looks reminiscent of a DS7, the rear and side profile look similar to a T-Roc, and the back end looks like a VW Tiguan! Ultimately though, it still looks smart. The large grille helps it stand out, and around the back there are cool-looking taillights and a full-length light bar. 

So while the outside may seem a bit all over the place, it’s a much more premium experience on the inside. There are soft-touch materials throughout the cabin, there are separate buttons for the climate control, and the infotainment & digital dials screen are clear and easy to use. There’s a whole load of storage throughout, and the front seats are pretty comfortable, even if they are missing lumbar support. 

Under the bonnet you’ll find a 1.5-litre hybrid petrol engine that can deliver an impressive 204hp! It has a top speed of 93mph, and can accelerate to 60mph in 8.9 seconds.

The Chery Tiggo 4 is a seriously affordable car. The entry-level starts at just £19,995, while the range-topper (that we have in this video) costs just £21,995! That seriously undercuts competitors like the Dacia Duster and Renault Captur, so is the Tiggo 4 the smart choice if you’re looking for a new car? Stick with Mat and see for yourself!

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-1506

videos.title: Can the Nuggets be the next Knicks?, with Jake Coyne | Serbian Corner
videos.summary_source_text:
Can the Nuggets be the next Knicks?, with Jake Coyne | Serbian Corner We have the new NBA champions!  The New York Knicks disposed of the San Antonio Spurs in 5 games, and maybe for the first time in 9 years, most pundits won't predict a repeat champion next season. Not because the Knicks aren't great, it's that we finally realise nobody's untouchable in today's NBA.

Jake Coyne of StatsPerform joins Miroslav to discuss whether we can predict what the next NBA champion is gonna look like. They also talk about whether the Nuggets are worth bringing back at a steep price, and which of the 10 offseason mocks Jake made is his favourite.

videos.title: Staal ➡️ Andersen: Freddie's emotional playoffs culminate in Cup lift! 🇩🇰
videos.summary_source_text:
Staal ➡️ Andersen: Freddie's emotional playoffs culminate in Cup lift! 🇩🇰 Hard work pays off! After starting every game of the Stanley Cup Playoffs until Game 5 of the Final for Carolina, Conn Smythe winner and captain Jordan Staal opts to give Frederik Andersen the Stanley Cup first. Andersen's emotional journey throughout these playoffs ends with him and teammate Nikolaj Ehlers as the second and third Danes to win the Stanley Cup after Lars Eller in 2018 with the Washington Capitals.

videos.title: MISSION COMPLETED!!! THE CAROLINA HURRICANES ARE THE 2026 STANLEY CUP CHAMPIONS!!!!
videos.summary_source_text:
MISSION COMPLETED!!! THE CAROLINA HURRICANES ARE THE 2026 STANLEY CUP CHAMPIONS!!!! THE CAROLINA HURRICANES ARE THE 2026 STANLEY CUP CHAMPIONS!!! They defeated the Vegas Golden Knights 3-0 in Game 6 to win their second-ever Stanley Cup in franchise history! Move over 2006; you have some company with the 2026 Hurricanes!!! On the Monday Stanley Cup Celebration Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the most complete Hurricanes win in the Stanley Cup Final, defeating the Golden Knights 3-0. Brandon Bussi earns his first playoff shutout, coming in a series-clincher. Taylor Hall and Jackson Blake had huge games to help lead the way. Nikolaj Ehlers with the exclamation point! Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on Jordan Staal winning the 2026 Conn Smythe. It makes sense to give it to the Captain after scoring six goals in the first five playoff games.  What does it mean for the captain and the Hurricanes? Finally, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts after seeing the Hurricanes win their second Stanley Cup in his life. He saw them do it in person in 2006, and now again 20 years later. What does it mean for the city? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: 🎙️ In Danish: Ehlers seals Stanley Cup victory with empty net goal, sets Hurricanes history! 🇩🇰
videos.summary_source_text:
🎙️ In Danish: Ehlers seals Stanley Cup victory with empty net goal, sets Hurricanes history! 🇩🇰 Nikolaj Ehlers does it again! The Danish star seals the Hurricanes' Stanley Cup victory over the Golden Knights when he fires home an empty net goal late in the 3rd period to put Carolina up 3-0. Andrei Svechnikov later hands Ehlers the Stanley Cup for the first time in his career as he enjoys his lap with Lord Stanley. Ehlers and teammate Frederik Andersen become just the 2nd and 3rd Danes to win the Stanley Cup, following Lars Eller in 2018 with the Washington Capitals. Ehlers also breaks his tie with Eric Staal and sets the new Hurricanes franchise record for points in a single Stanley Cup Final with 9! Listen to the Danish broadcast call Ehlers' crowning moment!

videos.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
videos.summary_source_text:
The Internet Is No Longer Human The internet just crossed a line most people missed. Bots now make up most web page requests, Google AI is answering questions before users click, and AI agents are starting to browse, shop, and act online for us.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

📌 What You’ll See:
Cloudflare data showing bots now outnumber humans on web pages
SOURCE: https://radar.cloudflare.com/traffic
Pew Research on how Google AI Overviews reduce website clicks
SOURCE: https://www.pewresearch.org/short-reads/2025/07/22/google-users-are-less-likely-to-click-on-links-when-an-ai-summary-appears-in-the-results/
Cloudflare’s Pay Per Crawl plan for charging AI crawlers
SOURCE: https://blog.cloudflare.com/introducing-pay-per-crawl/
Reuters on Penske suing Google over AI Overviews
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/boards-policy-regulation/rolling-stone-billboard-owner-penske-sues-google-over-ai-overviews-2025-09-14/
AP on Visa connecting payments to ChatGPT AI agents
SOURCE: https://apnews.com/article/d769dec86344cb4977c98789e8ec492f

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than bots. The web was built around humans visiting pages, reading content, clicking links, and buying things. Now AI is starting to read the web first, answer for it, browse through it, and even spend money inside it. The question is simple: are we still using the internet, or is the internet slowly being handed to machines?

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | RBC Canadian Open | Round 4 | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | RBC Canadian Open | Round 4 | 2026 Check out all the best shots from the 2026 RBC Canadian Open, featuring Tommy Fleetwood, Matt Fitzpatrick, Wyndham Clark and Bud Cauley, among others.

The PGA TOUR returns north of the border this week for the RBC Canadian Open, one of the longest-running events on the schedule and the third-oldest championship on the PGA TOUR calendar. TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley’s North Course hosts the national open for the second consecutive year, welcoming one of the strongest fields in tournament history as players make their final preparations for the U.S. Open. Four of the world’s top 10 players are set to tee it up, led by Matt Fitzpatrick, Justin Rose, Tommy Fleetwood and Collin Morikawa, who returns to the event for the first time since 2019. Recent PGA Championship winner Aaron Rai is back in action, while Brooks Koepka makes his first RBC Canadian Open appearance since 2019. Viktor Hovland adds further intrigue in his tournament debut, joining a field that also includes Robert MacIntyre, Sam Burns, Shane Lowry, Wyndham Clark, Tony Finau and Max Homa. Defending champion Ryan Fox returns looking to become the first player since Jhonattan Vegas to successfully defend the title. Home hopes will once again be high, with Corey Conners, Taylor Pendrith, Mackenzie Hughes and rookie Sudarshan Yellamaraju leading a strong Canadian contingent. Mike Weir is also set for his 33rd appearance in Canada’s national championship. In addition to 500 FedExCup points, a place in next week’s U.S. Open is available for the leading player not already exempt, adding another layer of significance to an already stacked championship. With a world-class field and plenty at stake, the RBC Canadian Open is set for another memorable week at TPC Toronto.

videos.title: Carolina One Win Away from the  Stanley Cup | Game 6 Preview & Conn Smythe Discussion
videos.summary_source_text:
Carolina One Win Away from the  Stanley Cup | Game 6 Preview & Conn Smythe Discussion The Stanley Cup will be in the building as the Carolina Hurricanes look to capture hockey's ultimate prize in Game 6 against the Vegas Golden Knights. Irfaan Gaffar, Jonny Lazarus, and David Pagnotta break down everything you need to know before puck drop, including whether Brandon Bussi can deliver another clutch performance with the Cup on the line and what lineup adjustments Vegas may need to make without William Karlsson.

The crew discusses the pressure facing the Golden Knights' biggest stars, including Jack Eichel, Mark Stone, Mitch Marner, and Tomas Hertl, as Vegas attempts to extend the series and force a winner-take-all Game 7. Plus, who deserves the Conn Smythe Trophy if Carolina finishes the job tonight? Jonny makes the case for Logan Stankoven and his playoff heroics, while David argues Jordan Staal's defensive dominance, faceoff excellence, and leadership make him the most valuable player of the Hurricanes' Stanley Cup run.

Can Carolina celebrate with the Stanley Cup in Las Vegas, or will the Golden Knights keep their championship hopes alive? The panel breaks down the biggest storylines, key matchups, and X-factors ahead of Game 6 of the Stanley Cup Final.

videos.title: What is Visceral Fat Really Does To the Body
videos.summary_source_text:
What is Visceral Fat Really Does To the Body Most people think body fat is all the same, but where fat is stored matters. The fat under the skin is very different from the fat that surrounds the organs, liver, pancreas, and even the heart itself. This visceral fat is closely linked to inflammation and metabolic dysfunction, which is why it's often associated with a higher risk of chronic disease. The real goal isn't simply losing weight, it's getting rid of the fat that was never meant to be there in the first place.

This is a clip from my conversation on The Primal Podcast. You can find the conversation on their channel or in the collaborations on my channel.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.


About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Cardiovascular Interventions, P.A. in Orlando Florida where, since 1990,  he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor performing thousands of interventional procedures in hospital and out patient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease . videos.title: This SHOCKED me...The Truth about Graphite Shafts
videos.summary_source_text:
This SHOCKED me...The Truth about Graphite Shafts For years, I believed graphite shafts were only for slower swing speeds, seniors, or golfers looking for more forgiveness.

Turns out, I was completely wrong.In this video, I put modern graphite shafts to the test and discovered some surprising performance benefits that many golfers are overlooking. From ball speed and dispersion to feel and consistency, the results challenged everything I thought I knew about shaft technology.

If you've always assumed steel shafts are the only option for serious golfers, this video might change your mind too.

In this video you'll learn:

The biggest myths about graphite shafts, How modern graphite technology has evolved, The performance differences between graphite and steel, Which golfers can benefit most from graphite shafts, Whether making the switch could help your game

Let me know in the comments. Have you ever tried graphite shafts, or are you still playing steel?

videos.title: GPT vs Grok: Who's Actually Winning the AGI Race? (Honest Scoreboard)
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT vs Grok: Who's Actually Winning the AGI Race? (Honest Scoreboard) Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Every week there's a new headline screaming that some model is "the smartest AI ever" and AGI is two years away. The charts contradict each other, the benchmarks are cherry-picked, and nobody measures the same thing twice.
So I skipped the hype reels and went through the actual technical reports, leaked parameter counts, benchmark numbers, and published roadmaps from OpenAI and xAI. The surprising part: the model winning the benchmark headlines isn't necessarily the one closest to AGI.
This is GPT vs Grok across the three things that actually matter — what's under the hood, what the benchmarks really say, and how close each one genuinely is to general intelligence. No hype, just the numbers we can verify.

Chapters:
00:00 The AI Headline Problem (Intro)
01:28 Model Architectures
03:34 Training Data & Compute
04:50 Benchmarks: GPT
06:08 Benchmarks: Grok
07:16 Key Takeaway on Benchmarks
08:00 AGI Indicators, Part 1: Generalization & Reasoning
09:28 AGI Indicators, Part 2: Tools, Learning & Safety
11:12 Summing Up the Indicators
11:41 Compute & Scaling
13:08 AGI Timeline Projections
14:02 The Probabilistic Forecast


If this breakdown saved you from another week of contradictory headlines, subscribe — skepticism is the whole point of this channel.

Who do you think cracks AGI first: the broad generalist or the brute-force specialist? Make your case in the comments. Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/

videos.title: The Main Factor Why Sidney Crosby Has Better Hockey IQ (It's Not What You Think)
videos.summary_source_text:
The Main Factor Why Sidney Crosby Has Better Hockey IQ (It's Not What You Think) Why Sidney Crosby Has Better Hockey IQ (It's Not What You Think)

Something to think about on hockey IQ which is actually one of the most misunderstood things in the sport. Most people think it's about being naturally smart on the ice when yes that is apart of it but it really comes down to mechanics. There are lots of smart hockey players out there, but none with the same IQ as Sidney Crosby. One reason is Crosby can actually use his full mental bandwidth and most players simply can't for a variety of mechanical related reasons.

In this video I break down the kinesiology of why trying harder actually makes you slower, what the "inner spring" is and how the best players in the world combine it with their active muscle contractions to move faster with less effort, and the turbo-boost mechanism inside your spinal cord that frees up your brain to scan the ice, read plays, and stay two steps ahead. Watch the full breakdown to see why mechanics, not IQ, is what lets Crosby play the way he does.

#train2point0 #downhillskating #hockeytraining #NHL sidney crosby, crosby hockey iq, hockey iq, hockey kinesiology, stretch shortening cycle hockey, inner spring hockey, hockey muscle contraction, jason yee hockey, train 2.0, downhill skating, why crosby is so good, hockey mental bandwidth, hockey skating mechanics, hockey movement science, NHL biomechanics, hockey performance, hockey reflex Why Sidney Crosby Has Better Hockey IQ (It's Not What You Think)

Something to think about on hockey IQ which is actually one of the most misunderstood things in the sport. Most people think it's about being naturally smart on the ice when yes that is apart of it but it really comes down to mechanics. There are lots of smart hockey players out there, but none with the same IQ as Sidney Crosby. One reason is Crosby can actually use his full mental bandwidth and most players simply can't for a variety of mechanical related reasons.

In this video I break down the kinesiology of why trying harder actually makes you slower, what the "inner spring" is and how the best players in the world combine it with their active muscle contractions to move faster with less effort, and the turbo-boost mechanism inside your spinal cord that frees up your brain to scan the ice, read plays, and stay two steps ahead. Watch the full breakdown to see why mechanics, not IQ, is what lets Crosby play the way he does.

Why Fasting May Be the Answer to Getting Rid of Stubborn Visceral Fat | @ThePrimal.Podcast Clip In this clip from my conversation on The Primal Podcast, I explain why visceral fat is one of the most dangerous types of fat for heart health. I’ll break down what actually causes visceral fat to build up, how it connects to inflammation and metabolic disease, and why fasting can be such a powerful tool for helping the body get rid of it.

Watch the full conversation here https://youtu.be/y5WeiL_fsMQ
Make sure to give it a like and subscribe to The Primal Podcast's channel!

On YouTube there are only two official channels:

@pradipjamnadasmd and @thegalenfoundation

There are numerous accounts, videos, and books circulating that use my image or claim to represent my work. In this age of AI, I encourage you to stay vigilant and take a moment to verify the source before believing or sharing any information not posted or confirmed through my official channels. videos.title: The Real Reason Your Eyesight Keeps Getting Worse
videos.summary_source_text:
The Real Reason Your Eyesight Keeps Getting Worse Digital eye strain can affect far more than just your eyes. Discover what happens to your eyes and brain after 8 hours of screen time, how screen use can contribute to myopia, and the best strategies for eye strain relief.

0:00 Digital eye strain
0:25 Myopia in children
1:18 Computer eye strain hour by hour 
3:25 Sunlight and your eyes
6:15 Peripheral vision and digital eye strain 
6:43 Nearsightedness after 8 hours of screen time
7:11 How to reduce eye strain
8:38 Daily sun for eye strain relief 

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/49YfcYr

Explore more ways to reduce digital eye strain and support healthy vision naturally:
▶️ https://youtu.be/nCnZX8zs4LI
▶️ https://youtu.be/CozZ1Rcm55E
▶️ https://youtu.be/oXhOK2880i8

Check out this article to learn about the most important nutrients for the eyes: https://drbrg.co/4xiUIDA

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Digital eye strain is becoming increasingly common as screen time continues to rise. Because the retina is actually brain tissue, prolonged screen use can affect both eye health and brain function.

The ciliary muscle controls the lens of the eye, allowing you to focus on objects that are near or far away. Extended screen time keeps this muscle contracted for hours at a time, making it difficult for the eyes to relax.

During the first few hours of screen use, you may not notice any symptoms. As screen time continues, your blink rate can drop significantly, reducing the lubrication of the eyes and contributing to digital eye strain.

After several hours of continuous screen use, the ciliary muscle can lose its ability to fully relax, similar to a muscle cramp. This may contribute to eye fatigue, difficulty focusing, dizziness, disorientation, reduced peripheral vision, and other symptoms associated with prolonged screen exposure.

One of the best ways to reduce eye strain and support eye health is to regularly look into the distance and spend time outdoors. Try leaving the house without your phone for 20 minutes and allow your gaze to naturally drift. Taking frequent breaks outside can help relax the eyes, support healthy vision, and may help reduce the risk of myopia.

videos.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
videos.summary_source_text:
This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5 Anthropic’s Fable 5 was live for only three days before everything changed. The US government stepped in, access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 was shut down, and now the AI world is facing a huge question: what happens when frontier AI becomes too powerful for public access?

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

📌 What You’ll See:
Anthropic’s official statement on shutting down Fable 5 and Mythos 5
SOURCE: https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access
WIRED on the US government order and the Fable 5 shutdown
SOURCE: https://www.wired.com/story/anthropic-says-us-government-ordered-it-to-shut-down-mythos-models/
Anthropic’s launch post for Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
SOURCE: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5
TechCrunch on Anthropic’s safety warnings and the government crackdown
SOURCE: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/12/anthropics-safety-warnings-may-have-just-backfired-the-government-has-pulled-the-plug-on-its-most-powerful-ai/
Reuters on the US order limiting foreign access to Anthropic’s top models
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/technology/us-blocks-foreign-access-anthropics-most-advanced-ai-models-axios-reports-2026-06-13/

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than one AI model. If Fable 5 can be shut down after launch, every powerful frontier model could face the same risk. The big question is simple: will regular people still get access to the strongest AI systems, or will the best models disappear behind government walls?

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | RBC Canadian Open | Round 3 | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | RBC Canadian Open | Round 3 | 2026 Check out all the best shots from Round 3 of the 2026 RBC Canadian Open, featuring Tommy Fleetwood, Wyndham Clark, Shane Lowry and Billy Horschel, among others.

The PGA TOUR returns north of the border this week for the RBC Canadian Open, one of the longest-running events on the schedule and the third-oldest championship on the PGA TOUR calendar. TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley’s North Course hosts the national open for the second consecutive year, welcoming one of the strongest fields in tournament history as players make their final preparations for the U.S. Open. Four of the world’s top 10 players are set to tee it up, led by Matt Fitzpatrick, Justin Rose, Tommy Fleetwood and Collin Morikawa, who returns to the event for the first time since 2019. Recent PGA Championship winner Aaron Rai is back in action, while Brooks Koepka makes his first RBC Canadian Open appearance since 2019. Viktor Hovland adds further intrigue in his tournament debut, joining a field that also includes Robert MacIntyre, Sam Burns, Shane Lowry, Wyndham Clark, Tony Finau and Max Homa. Defending champion Ryan Fox returns looking to become the first player since Jhonattan Vegas to successfully defend the title. Home hopes will once again be high, with Corey Conners, Taylor Pendrith, Mackenzie Hughes and rookie Sudarshan Yellamaraju leading a strong Canadian contingent. Mike Weir is also set for his 33rd appearance in Canada’s national championship. In addition to 500 FedExCup points, a place in next week’s U.S. Open is available for the leading player not already exempt, adding another layer of significance to an already stacked championship. With a world-class field and plenty at stake, the RBC Canadian Open is set for another memorable week at TPC Toronto.

videos.title: Nej, alla åker inte på utlandssemester varje sommar
videos.summary_source_text:
Nej, alla åker inte på utlandssemester varje sommar Alla åker inte på utlandssemester. Alla har inte sommarstuga. 

Jag påminner om att tjugo procent av den svenska befolkningen inte klarar en oförutsedd utgift på 15 000 kronor — och att det vi ser på sociala medier är andras höjdpunkter, inte deras vardag. Samma survivorship bias som i investeringsvärlden, fast applicerad på sommaren.

Mer på rikatillsammans.se — länk i pinnad kommentar.

#shorts #privatekonomi #sommar #ekonomi #familj #survivorshipbias Alla åker inte på utlandssemester. Alla har inte sommarstuga.

videos.title: STOP PUSHING! You're Damaging Your Colon | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
STOP PUSHING! You're Damaging Your Colon | Dr. Mandell Discover why pushing too hard during a bowel movement may be doing more harm than good. In this video, Dr. Mandell explains how excessive straining can increase your risk of hemorrhoids, pelvic floor problems, anal fissures, and other complications while actually working against your body's natural reflexes. Learn simple techniques to improve bowel movements naturally, including proper toilet positioning, hydration, fiber-rich foods, movement, breathing, and healthy habits that support your colon. These easy changes can make a big difference in your digestive health and help you work with your body instead of against it.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: Is Gemini 4 AGI? What Google Has Really Shipped
videos.summary_source_text:
Is Gemini 4 AGI? What Google Has Really Shipped Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone's selling you a "Gemini 4 LEAKED" thumbnail. The problem? As of right now, Gemini 4 doesn't exist in a single official Google channel — no blog post, no API entry, no dated release note. In this video we skip the recycled rumors and go straight to the source: Google's developer docs, the I/O 2026 keynote, and the actual API catalog. Then we line up the Gemini line against the rivals it'll have to beat, and answer the question behind every clickbait title — is this thing AGI?

By the end you'll be able to spot a fake Gemini 4 leak in two seconds flat.

We cover what Google has actually shipped (the Gemini 3.5 series), the release cadence everyone's reading the tea leaves on, the credible signals (including Demis Hassabis on the record), where Gemini sits against OpenAI and Anthropic on context and modality, what a real Gemini 4 would change for research, products, and enterprise — and a clear-eyed look at the AGI question and the risks that ride along with every capability jump.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 — The hype problem (and what's really there)
1:09 — The Official Word: what Google has shipped
3:10 — Rumors & Expectations: the informed bets
5:11 — How It Stacks Up Against the Competition
6:52 — What a Real Gemini 4 Would Actually Change
8:34 — Is Gemini 4 AGI?
9:57 — The Risks Nobody Should Skip
11:09 — The Bottom Line

🔎 What did you already see floating around as a "Gemini 4 release date"? Drop it in the comments — let's see how many of these so-called leaks contradict each other.

👍 If you're sick of the hype cycle, a like helps the channel reach more people who want the no-hype version.
🔔 Subscribe — the second Google posts the real model card and benchmarks, I'm covering it here first.

videos.title: What Are We Really Eating?
videos.summary_source_text:
What Are We Really Eating? For thousands of years, food came directly from nature. A plant came from the ground, fruit came from a tree, and meat came from a single animal. Today, many food products are created by combining ingredients from countless different sources into something entirely new. The more food becomes a product instead of a food, the more important it becomes to ask what we are really eating.

This is a clip from my video "How to Make Dinner the Feast Your Body is Craving"

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Is Getting Roasted — Here's Why
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Fable 5 Is Getting Roasted — Here's Why Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/

You're paying for an AI assistant every month — but is it the RIGHT one for your work? In 2026, "Best AI" isn't a simple answer anymore, so here's the most honest, hype-free breakdown of Anthropic's brand-new Claude Fable 5.


In this video we cover: Anthropic's official launch story — the 1-million-token context window, $10/$50 pricing, and the introduction of the new Mythos class. Real developer reactions — from Simon Willison to hands-on coding benchmarks (80.3% on SWE-Bench Pro, beating both Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5). 


The controversy that dominated headlines — a mandatory 30-day data retention policy, even for enterprise customers who previously had zero-retention agreements. And the biggest twist: a Wired report exposing Anthropic's quiet plan to "silently degrade" outputs for competing AI builders — and the public reversal that followed within just 48 hours.


We break down the difference between Fable 5 and Mythos 5, how the safety classifiers actually work, the false positives developers are running into, and whether the price hike is justified or not.

Japan is rebuilding its entire crypto framework to allow regulated ETFs, Hong Kong is restricting licenses to incumbent players, and Korea’s banks are buying exchanges as retail interest fades.
